text.title: Minimum Product Frameworks Core Rules
text.summary: Selection matrix that chooses one of nine 'minimum product' frameworks (MVP, MLP, MMP, MAC, RAT, MDP, MVA, MFP, SLC) by market density × ICP × differentiator BEFORE the first spec.md exists.

rule.statement: The chosen framework MUST be recorded as a decision-record (DR) with three signals: market density, buyer type, differentiator.
rule.rationale: Without a written DR, the team drifts to default-MVP regardless of fit; DR forces explicit signal evaluation.

rule.statement: The matrix MUST evaluate all nine frameworks (MVP, MLP, MMP, MAC, RAT, MDP, MVA, MFP, SLC). Skipping a row produces invisible bias toward whichever framework the operator already knows.

rationale.source: Henrik Kniberg / Marty Cagan minimum-product taxonomy

rule.rationale: Familiar-framework bias is the dominant mis-pick; full-matrix forcing function neutralises it.

rule.statement: The DR MUST name what would prove the chosen differentiator (price -> unit economics; experience -> NPS+activation; capability -> feature-eval win; distribution -> channel CAC).
rule.rationale: Differentiators without proof gates degrade into marketing copy.

rule.statement: DR MUST list 2-3 quantitative triggers that would force a framework re-pick (activation < X, churn > Y, MRR flat 90 days).
rule.rationale: Without triggers, sunk-cost keeps the wrong framework; pre-registered triggers force reassessment.

rule.statement: The framework choice describes HOW you ship, not WHAT you ship. Mixing them (e.g., 'MLP for X feature') is a category error.
rule.rationale: Conflating framework with feature scope produces incoherent specs.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — DR already exists <=90 days; consulting/services; compliance-driven build; iteration on stable product), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
